subject: UX & Research
title: Information Scent
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Information Scent.

hook.text: Users follow links like animals following a scent trail. Each click either strengthens or weakens the trail.

buildup.text: Pirolli and Card coined 'information scent' in 1999. Users assess links by how likely they seem to lead to the goal.

discovery.text: Strong scent means labels clearly match intent. 'Track your order' has strong scent for someone checking shipping.

twist.text: Weak scent makes users hesitate. 'Services' could mean anything. Users don't click links they can't predict.

climax.text: Make every link a clear promise. The text should predict exactly what the user will find after clicking.

punchline.text: Strong scent: users click confidently. Weak scent: they hesitate and leave.

quiz.question: What makes a link have 'strong information scent'?
options: The label clearly matches what the user expects to find after clicking
The link is styled in a bright color with a large font
The link appears at the top of the page
